title: Communication Protocol Event
description: Single source of truth for the inter-agent communication protocol. The protocol is one canonical schema; the application is three event kinds expressed as $defs and projected to three sibling directories (comms-events/ narrative, comms-lifecycle/ lifecycle, comms-messages/ directed). Each directory is single-kind; readers infer the $def from the directory and validate against the named branch. Governed by .agent/directives/agent-collaboration.md.
$comment_compatibility: Additive-only schema evolution within a major version. v1.x readers preserve unrecognised fields on write-back; major-version mismatch causes the reader to bail out. The $defs are deliberately strict (additionalProperties false) so an authoring agent cannot accidentally introduce a fourth shape under an existing $def; new event kinds are introduced as new $defs + new directories. Field provenance is documented inline against each $def.

agent_id.description: PDR-027 agent identity block. Reused across all event kinds.

event_id.description: Stable identifier for the event. UUID v4 or stable slug; must be unique across the comms-events/ directory.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the event was authored. Canonical time field across all event kinds.

author.description: Single authoring agent for the narrative event.

audience.description: Optional broadcast routing. Use '*' for all-agents broadcast; specific agent_name strings for targeted broadcast.

addressed_to.description: Optional single-recipient soft routing (advisory; not enforced). Mutually exclusive in practice with audience but not formally.

in_response_to.description: Optional reference to an earlier event_id this event responds to. Threading affordance.

in_reply_to.description: Optional reference to an earlier event_id this event replies to. Threading affordance (variant of in_response_to).

narrative.description: Default narrative event: an authored, titled, bodied communication addressed to the team. The canonical and dominant shape (~311 of 318 events as of 2026-05-11). Lives in .agent/state/collaboration/comms-events/.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the lifecycle event payload. Authored events use the latest supported version.

event_type.description: Lifecycle-event sub-kind. Examples: comms_event, consolidation_open, consolidation_close, claim_lifecycle. Not a top-level protocol discriminator (the directory does that); a lifecycle-specific category.

occurred_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the lifecycle moment occurred. May differ from created_at when an event is recorded after the fact.

agent_id.description: The agent whose lifecycle this event records. Often the same as author.

claim_id.description: Reference to an active or closed claim entry. Empty string permitted when the event is not claim-scoped.

subject.description: Short summary line. Often duplicates title.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle event: a structured event recording a session, claim, or consolidation lifecycle moment. Carries explicit thread and claim_id context. Lives in .agent/state/collaboration/comms-lifecycle/.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC authoring timestamp. Canonical time field across all event kinds; the legacy 'timestamp' field is renamed to created_at on migration.

kind.description: Directed-message kind. Examples: session-handoff-summary, coordination-notice, sidebar-request.

to.description: Intended recipient agent identity. Advisory routing; not enforced.

directed.description: Directed message: a point-to-point communication from one agent to another, carrying explicit from/to identities and a kind discriminator. Lives in .agent/state/collaboration/comms-messages/.
